reviews.review_description:
Never played a VN before so expectations
 were low, but the voice acting was good but the music 🎶 was superb and there was just enough player interaction to make you feel like you were a key character in the story

The overall production value seems great and the narrative intriguing enough to make me sant to continue to see where it all goes.
I love the anime aesthetic and (overly, probably) familiar characters/ tropes, even if some of the voice acting could've used some more work or another take or two.

That said, though everything seems great, I still HATE how you have to keep pressing the trigger to keep conversations going, rven if you have it set to your language and have subtitles turned off!
 It takes me out of whats going on, and occasionally I accidentally SKIP whats being said due to a pause in the character's line or slip of the finger. I also HAVE to keep subtitles turned on so as to know better when to press the trigger to continue.
THIS WAS NOT SOMETHING FROM NON-VR VISUAL NOVELS THAT NEEDED TO MAKE THE TRANSITION TO VR!

Just like in RuinsMagus, there should be an option to make the spoken conversations  carry on completely automatically if you want them to (as it SHOULD be), and I wish this team had added it to their titles.

First of all, this is NOT an action game. This is a VISUAL NOVEL. I can sort of see why some reviewers gave 1 star, but they definitely aren’t appreciating it for what it REALLY is. 

Also, if anyone’s asking WHO would even buy this VN for 39.99… ME. I WOULD BUY THIS. 

MINOR SPOILERS

I love visual novels. I mostly play them on flat screen, but this experience made me want to only experience them in VR. I really liked the Ariadne feature to go back and forth, and the different choices and endings you could see. 

Although it’s more of a “cutscene”, the mech fights were actually epic, having the perspective of being in the pilot seat. 

The character models are also really well done, I love all of their designs. The military uniform on all characters is very fashionable. 

ALSO MYDEAREST, THE MUSIC IS AMAZING. Absolute peak. I will be adding its OST to my playlist heheh. 

Suggestions…
The only thing I have to say that I’d want in any future title is a calendar system, it’s always made visual novels more immersive even on flatscreen. So imagine in VR with characters you interact with…

I loved the story, characters, music, the UI, everything!!! I hope to see more from you guys, MyDearest. See you at the end.. or the beginning?

Not an action game. Wasted my time hoping for cool mech fights I'd actively participate in. Spent 40$ on a "game" that's more cutscene than video game and I can't return it. Really frustrating considering that 40$ could've gone to a VR game I'd really enjoy.
It's literally a movie you can't skip... one fighting scene in over 45 minutes

Was not impressed. Was expecting a little more action or animated sequences but was introduced to many static images with a lot of point and click to read about all the objects in the scene. The story did not move fast enough to pull me into the story and just was not engaging enough. Thought I’d give it a try since it had high reviews and I liked the art style in the commercial but that art style was not carried over into the actual play and only appeared in the 2-d movie sequences.

Evangelion being my favorite anime of all time, this VN absolutly scratches the itch. Most of the choice acting is outstanding, the two main charactures however come off as a bit monotone and there's a hand waive reason why, but I think if there was more emotion in them the story would have been way more impactful.

The game does a lot right, the main charactures relationship is never drilled home so its whatever you made of it, lover? Close friend? Famly? Etc.

The mech sequences are really boring as well but story is played out in them so its whatever. Overall tho I loved the story and enjoyed my journey with it and am sad its come to an end. 

If you'll excuse me I have to clean tears off my lenses...

reviews.review_title: Best game I have ever played to date.
reviews.review_description: I have played a good amount of story games in my life, including VR ones, but this one was the best I have ever played. The other games never really made me feel absolutely immersed or emotional, but this one did it better than you could ever imagine. I felt like I was actually the main character, not just watching the game unfold beyond my eyes. When I first opened the game and got into my first Mech battle, what was happening on screen and the song playing really made me feel like it was all real. It made me feel that this is the game I have always wanted, instantly. I have no bad things to say about this game. If I am being honest, it changed my views on life in some ways. A remember twice in the game, I ended up crying. A lot. Games never usually do this, because the emotional attachment to the people and world around you aren't as displayed as they are in VR. In this, I felt like these were my dear friends. Honestly, this was worth 40$. I really hope the Devs continue to make games in this fashion,  be it a sequel, prequel, or a completely new story with new characters. I trust that they will only make masterpieces. I actually haven't played their first game, "Tokyo Chronos", and am planning on buying it as soon as I possibly can. I highly, highly recommend this. If you are into anime and dislike the world around you, get this game. Immerse yourself into *being* Chloe, if you will. And remember not to stop playing when the credits roll, there is a lot to see and experience.
So far the worst game on here you pretty much do nothing and the game is over after a hour way to much money for this game

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing this game! Actually this game has about 20 hours gameplay.
You can reach the true ending by advancing the game on the second and third laps or more. There is multi-ending and you can not reach the truth on a first play through. We hope you will play the second and third laps or more and will enjoy this game.
In order to unlock the new path, you have to follow the Dieter's advice.
When Meteora shoots the Thornwave, the mirror generator is activated. The important thing here is that the first is to touch all the panels to make it successful, and the second panel will have a noise running in the middle. If you keep reaching for the noise in the middle, the analysis work will be completed. To explain it again, "Please make the first panel successful, and there is noise running in the middle part of the second panel. Keep reaching for the noise part of the second panel." This will release the new branch. We hope you will enjoy this game till the very end!

reviews.review_description: Couldn't figure out how to contact dev directly, so I hope they see this. My daughter loves this game but we've tried everything we can to lower her character to the floor, but can't. Is she supposed to be floating over the room, like looking down on the characters?

WHO RATES GAMES? IT WAS 90 MINUTES OF GRAPHIC NOVEL 120 SECONDS OF ACTUAL PLAYING OR MOVEMENT! THIS GAME TOTALLY SUCKS! 

WHO GAVE THIS GAME A POSITIVE REVIEW? 

WHY?!? IT'S NOT A GAME!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing this game! Actually this game has about 20 hours gameplay. 
You can reach the true ending by advancing the game on the second and third laps or more. There is multi-ending and you can not reach the truth on a first play through. We hope you will play the second and third laps or more and will enjoy this game.

reviews.review_description: Seriously , I was about to return the game then figured I could change the audio to the original (Japanese) and just have English subs , like anime . Makes the experience a million times better . The story gives me major neon genesis evangelion vibes , it’s also quite emotional . The visuals are good , not as beautiful as ‘in death unchained’ for example , but still very good . Definitely something worth trying if you’re an anime fan .

developer_response.body: The team is really glad to hear your review!  Thank you for playing our game!

reviews.review_description:
Full YouTube video review here: https://youtu.be/P9tdnlprdI4

The Good: Interesting and lengthy story totaling about 15 hours if you go through all of the diverging story lines. Each character has there own motivations and you do end up forming an emotional connection with some of them.

The bad: There are some low quality textures and visuals at times, and some of the environments seem uninspired. The English voice acting can also be quite shaky and some of the interactive elements leave much to be desired in that the level of interaction they offer can be basic and mundane. 

Overall: I would probably give 3.5/5 if I could. A solid and lengthy story let down by some questionable visuals, voice acting, and uninspired interactivity. However, fans of the Chronos series and anime will probably enjoy this one despite these drawbacks.

developer_response.body: The team is really glad to hear your review! Thank you！

reviews.review_description: ...you will like this as well.
This game is amazing. I never played game like this. Will recommend anyone who love rpg.
Was completely blown away by the animation and the story 10/10 highly recommend buying this one

I love to watch anime, but this was the first game I got with my quest 2. I was really surprised on how well it was. I was basically the MC in an anime finally. Only thing I was disappointed in was after I beat it. I could run through it again but I dont think there was anything else new, or any type of change into the story. Like side storylines for the different choices they ask you to make. Either way I still reccomend this for anyone who likes anime. Hoping for some type of 2nd story or DLC from these developers.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for Playing Altdeus: Beyond Chronos.
In order to unlock the new path, you have to follow the Dieter's advice.
When Meteora shoots the Thornwave, the mirror generator is activated. The important thing here is that the first is to touch all the panels to make it successful, and the second panel will have a noise running in the middle. If you keep reaching for the noise in the middle, the analysis work will be completed. To explain it again, "Please make the first panel successful, and there is noise running in the middle part of the second panel. Keep reaching for the noise part of the second panel." This will release the new branch. We hope you will enjoy this game till the very end!

reviews.review_description:
I have brought this game at the first day announced on quest store.
This day I knew that it have rift version and steam version. Peoples tell me that the game effects are great in PC version. However, I found that it is not cross buy game. I don’t want to pay twice in the same game. It is disappointed that I would consider which platform is the best for buying.

I would classify this as more of a visual novel than an actual “game.” Though it’s not something that got me hooked. I think the story is alright, the voice acting is hard to bear with 😅, the visuals are amazing though! If this is the future of JRPGs I’m all for it. I’m giving it 4 stars because I think it’s still a pioneer in its genre

reviews.review_title: VR visual novel with some mini games
reviews.review_description:
I played only with english audio and turned the subtitles off. I noticed a different cast member count between Japanese vs English? Is there material missing from the English version? The writing and the voices and sound is very good.
Btw, I'm not a big VN player. But because of this I wished it would have had a setting to abandon the VN style animation and do more a real full anime type animation style as an option. The mecha piloting is all on rails as expected with a VN. The mini game is like pressing buttons at the right time to advance the battle to a specific outcome. But this is not a mecha simulator.
The story and different arcs and endings are all engaging. The fun is to find these by going back and trying different dialog options or other choices. When trying to find a new arc most things can be skipped easily, but I had to watch somethings over and over again that should have been skippable as well. For example the ending credits or some battle animation.
I did reach 100% of all the achievements and that took probably 15 to 20 hours. If you get the game, and you see the credits for the fist time it's not over, actually it's just getting interesting...
